title: Mean-imputation dilution defeats leak-corr gate for columns with any NaN
detail: Corr filter mean-imputes NaN cells then computes |corr| vs var_y over ALL rows; computed corr = corr_true*sqrt(1-f) for NaN fraction f. With default forbidden_base_corr_threshold=0.99999, f>2e-5 (~2 NaN rows per 100k) lets an exact y-copy pass; it then wins auto-base MI ranking -> composite built on y-derived base. Pre-vectorisation per-column _safe_corr caught this. Float32 dot accumulation (~1e-6..1e-5 error) compounds vs the 1e-5 threshold band.
fix: Renormalise per column by sqrt(var_y_finite_j/var_y_all) via one masked matmul, or route NaN-bearing columns through scalar _safe_corr (per-pair masking); do corr reductions in float64. Regression test: y-copy with 1% NaN must be dropped.

title: iter_transform crashes on auto-promoted linear_residual_multi specs
detail: iter_transform extracts only spec.base_column (1-D); for multi-base specs (multi_base_enabled=True by DEFAULT) _linear_residual_multi_forward raises ValueError 'base has 1 columns but fitted alphas has K entries' (linear.py:364). All other consumers (ensemble/__init__.py:439,674,879) already stack (base_column,)+extra_base_columns; this public API was missed. No test covers it.
fix: Build cols=(base_column,)+extra_base_columns; np.column_stack when K>1 and pass the (n,K) matrix to domain_check/forward (both accept 2-D). Add multi-base iter_transform regression test.

title: Multi-base promotion fits joint OLS on NaN-unfiltered rows; silently dead with lag bases
detail: _pool_arrays/_y_train_local are raw train rows; _linear_residual_multi_fit has no finite masking. K>=2 with NaN: SVD LinAlgError -> cond=inf -> collinear fallback (alphas=0) -> every stepwise trial degenerates -> promotion never fires. K=1 seed with NaN: lstsq raises -> caught -> single-base kept. Lag bases (canonical) have leading NaN in train, so the default-ON, benchmark-validated 83% win is silently lost; final fit at 738 is outside the try and can also poison params.
fix: Mask rows to isfinite(y)&all(isfinite(base_matrix),axis=1) in forward_stepwise._cv_rmse_with_folds and before the final _linear_residual_multi_fit (mirror _linear_residual_multi_domain). Biz-value test: promotion fires on y=b1+b2+eps where b1 has a leading NaN.

title: Hint-strength gate misaligned after hint drops (positional slice of raw strengths)
detail: _hint_strengths_pct aligns with the RAW ablation hint list (core/_phase_composite_discovery.py:346), but the gate uses max(hint_strengths[:len(hint_kept)]) after filtered entries are removed. If the strong top-1 hint is dropped (e.g. by the corr filter), its 501% strength is attributed to the surviving weak hints -> full-hint trust + demoter/dedup immunity granted wrongly.
fix: Plumb strengths as {feature: delta_pct} (or zip+filter pairs together); gate on max(strength[c] for c in hint_kept).

title: Alpha-drift z-score omits sqrt(2) for difference of two independent half-estimates
detail: Var(a1-a2)=2*sigma^2/(half*var_base) but se_alpha=sigma_resid/(sqrt(half)*base_std) is the single-estimate SE -> z inflated 1.41x; default threshold 3.0 acts like ~2.12 sigma -> excess drift WARNINGs and over-rejection when reject_on_alpha_drift=True. ENS-Low-2 fixed sigma, not this factor.
fix: se_alpha = sigma_resid*sqrt(2)/(sqrt(half)*base_std); update tests pinning z values.

title: Stratified sampler truncation drops the highest (latest) row indices
detail: per_stratum over n_strata+1 bins (extra NaN-y bin) overshoots sample_n; out.sort(); out[:sample_n] deletes overshoot exclusively from the LARGEST indices = most recent rows, contradicting the docstring's temporal-preservation rationale. With heavy-tail boost (n_strata 10->30, default-on) + NaN-y, ~3.3k of 100k latest rows excised. Also undershoots sample_n via floor division when no overshoot.
fix: Drop the excess via seeded rng.choice(out, sample_n, replace=False) BEFORE the final sort; distribute the sample_n % n_strata remainder across strata.

title: fit_with_stability_check mutates shared config random_state; restore lost after heavy-tail model_copy
detail: Suite passes the shared config object into discovery uncopied (no-hint path). Stability loop writes self.config.random_state in place (visible to other consumers mid-run); if the heavy-tail boost swaps self.config for a model_copy during run i, the line-223 restore writes to the copy and the ORIGINAL config keeps base_seed+i*7919 permanently, silently changing other targets' seeds and cache-key signatures.
fix: self.config = self.config.model_copy() once at entry; restore by re-assigning the saved original object, not by writing the field.

title: report_ kept-flags frozen at eps gate; drift/collapse/rerank/multi-base drops invisible
detail: entry['kept']=True set at line 477; specs later removed by alpha-drift reject (569), linres->diff collapse (631), tiny rerank (655) or replaced by multi-base upgrades (740-760, new names absent from report_) still show kept=True, reason='' in report(), contradicting its documented contract and misleading audits of missing specs.
fix: After kept_specs is final, reconcile: kept=False + reason='dropped_by_<gate>' for entries absent from the final list; add synthetic entries for upgraded names with upgraded_from.

title: Duplicate multi-base specs when two seeds converge to the same base set
detail: Seed X adding Y gives [X,Y]; seed Y adding X gives [Y,X]; both upgraded with distinct names ('+'.join order differs) for the SAME joint-OLS transform. No set-level dedup after the upgrade loop -> 2x downstream training for one composite and two perfectly correlated ensemble members.
fix: Dedup _upgraded_specs on (transform_name, frozenset((base_column,)+extra_base_columns)), keeping the higher-ranked spec.

title: Per-base np.delete copies (float32+int64) held for ALL bases; mi_y recomputed per base
detail: Each base context stores np.delete copies of the full screen matrix AND prebinned int64 matrix (~12 B/cell), all alive simultaneously (~6 GB at 100k x 500 x 10 bases). With default aggregation='mean', mi_y_for_base is derivable from ONE _mi_per_feature_y_fixed pass: (S - mi_j)/(F-1), yet a full (F-1)-column MI pass runs per base.
fix: Compute the per-feature MI(y,x_j) vector once and derive per-base means arithmetically; give _mi_to_target_prebinned an exclude_col param (skip j in its loop) instead of np.delete - zero copies, bit-identical. Keep np.delete only for the knn path.

title: _mi_to_target knn path: global AND finite mask lets one NaN-heavy column zero all MI
detail: finite = isfinite(target) & all(isfinite(X), axis=1): a column with >=50 finite rows (passes _filter_features) but 99% NaN collapses rows below 50 -> return 0.0 for every mi_y/mi_t -> all gains 0 -> discovery silently degenerates with mi_estimator='knn' (the heavy-tail-accurate option). Same bug class fixed for the prebinned path in Wave 24 (screening.py:306-313) but not here.
fix: Per-column finite handling for the knn estimator (mirror the prebinned sentinel design), or drop >X%-NaN columns before the AND-mask and log row shrinkage.

title: Auto-base AND-mask silently shrinks ranking sample with MNAR bias for mid-NaN columns
detail: Columns with 10-90% NaN pass the 10%-finite drop; the all-column AND mask then intersects their NaN patterns (5 independent 50%-NaN cols leave ~3% rows). If >=50 rows survive, MI ranking + demoters + dedup all run on a tiny rows-where-everything-observed subsample (MNAR selection bias) with no signal; the mean-impute fallback engages only below 50 rows.
fix: Log shrinkage when finite.sum() < 0.5*n_screen; for the bin estimator fall back to per-column _mi_pair_bin (per-pair masks) when the AND-mask retains <~50% of rows.

title: Unary transforms scored against an arbitrary first base's X-without-base context
detail: Unary dedup binds each requires_base=False transform to the FIRST base in candidate order; eval computes its mi_gain against that base's x_remaining (a column the transform never uses) and stamps base_column=<first base> into the spec name. The gain thus changes with auto-base ranking order (an irrelevant degree of freedom) and the spec claims a nonexistent base dependence.
fix: Dedicated unary context: x_remaining=_full_x_matrix (+prebinned), mi_y computed once on the full matrix, base_column=''/sentinel rendered base-free in spec/name.

title: _extract_column_array crashes on pandas nullable dtypes containing NA
detail: df[col].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32) on nullable Int64/Float64/boolean with pd.NA raises ValueError ('cannot convert ... with missing values'); _is_numeric_column approves those dtypes (line 92), so Arrow-backed/nullable frames crash discovery at the first column pull instead of mapping NA->NaN.
fix: Pass na_value=np.nan to to_numpy (branch on extension dtype if needed).

title: Boolean-mask train_idx accepted silently, detonates later with cryptic IndexError
detail: np.asarray accepts a bool mask (common numpy idiom); y_full[mask] works but _sample_indices uses mask LENGTH as row count and train_idx[sample_idx]/base_train[sample_idx] index positionally into mask/short arrays -> IndexError far from the misuse, naming neither train_idx nor the cause (or bool reinterpreted as indices 0/1 when mi_sample_n=None).
fix: At fit entry: if train_idx.dtype == bool: train_idx = np.flatnonzero(train_idx); reject float dtypes with a clear TypeError. Same for val_idx/test_idx.

title: No train/val/test disjointness or bounds validation despite documented leakage discipline
detail: fit stores val_idx_/test_idx_ 'for later integrity checks' but performs none: overlapping train/test indices (easy caller bug after re-splitting) silently fit transform params and MI screens on test rows. Duplicate train indices (bias MI) and out-of-bounds indices also unchecked. Check cost is O(n log n) - negligible vs MI screening.
fix: Raise on non-empty np.intersect1d(train_idx, val/test_idx); warn on duplicated train_idx; bounds-check max index vs len(df). Unit test asserting the raise.

title: Bootstrap-failure warning fires only when replicate 0 fails, not on first failure
detail: Comment says 'log first per-spec failure' but the guard is `if b == 0:` - failures at replicates 1..N-1 are silent and the LCB is computed over a reduced/biased bootstrap sample with no operator signal (the >= bootstrap_n//2 guard only catches extreme under-sampling).
fix: Use a _warned flag set on first exception; include the running failure count in the message.

title: y_full[train_idx] materialised three times per fit
detail: y_train (219), y_train_for_strat (288) and _y_train_local (696, with a dead 'if y_full is not None' else-branch) are the same fancy-index copy: three O(n_train) allocations+gathers where one suffices.
fix: Reuse y_train for all three; delete the dead else-branch at line 696.

title: float(np.std(empty)) or 1.0 is NaN-truthy in the linres->diff collapse
detail: All-NaN y_train makes np.std return NaN; bool(NaN) is True so 'or 1.0' never engages -> std_y=NaN -> alpha_dev/beta_dev NaN -> collapse silently disabled. Practically unreachable today but a guard idiom that invites copy-paste.
fix: Explicit guard: if not (isfinite(std_y) and std_y > 0): std_y = 1.0.

title: _df_ref pins the full frame on the instance; no __getstate__ exclusion
detail: Fitted discovery holds a hard reference to the (possibly 100+GB) training frame for iter_transform; pickling/deep-copying the instance serialises the whole frame and the ref pins it against GC for the instance lifetime. _auto_base_pool similarly holds n_train-sized arrays per base.
fix: Add __getstate__/__setstate__ dropping _df_ref and _auto_base_pool; document that iter_transform requires its explicit df argument after unpickling.

title: Near-duplicates of the base in X_remaining attenuate mi_gain (conservative bias)
detail: np.delete removes only the base's own column; a 0.99-correlated sibling (lag2/smoothed copy) inflates mi_y_compare while adding little to mi_t (T residualised against the base), biasing gain DOWN for exactly the lag families most likely to be genuine. No leak, but unfair cross-base comparison; auto-base dedup filters candidates, not the screening feature set.
fix: Optionally exclude columns with |corr(base,x_j)| >= auto_base_dedup_corr_threshold from that base's x_remaining via a per-base column mask (combines with the D10 exclude-col refactor at zero copies).

title: 'Explicit base outside the FE pool' branch is unreachable
detail: _resolve_base_candidates filters explicit bases to usable_features (__init__.py:344) and _auto_base returns subsets of it, so every base is in _col_index; the else-arm assigning the FULL matrix as x_remaining (which would also wrongly keep the base in its own 'remaining' set) cannot execute.
fix: Delete or convert to a loud logger.error guard; note the load-bearing invariant in _resolve_base_candidates.

title: Degenerate-sample fallback ignores hint priority
detail: When <50 finite rows survive even after impute, the fallback returns list(usable_features)[:top_k] in arbitrary column order, discarding hint_kept - the one signal (BD ablation) independent of the broken screening sample and treated as authoritative everywhere else.
fix: return (hint_kept + [c for c in usable_features if c not in hint_kept])[:top_k].

title: For 50 <= n < 5*mi_nbins all bin-MI is silently 0; ranking degenerates to name order
detail: fit warns below 50 rows, but _mi_pair_bin/_mi_to_target_prebinned return 0.0 below 5*nbins rows (80 at default nbins=16). In the 50-79 band every mi_gain is exactly 0; with default eps_mi_gain=-10 all candidates pass tied and top-K selection falls to the alphabetical tiebreaker. Rerank arbitrates, but the MI 'ranking' is fictitious and undocumented in this band.
fix: Log when min(train_idx.size, mi_sample_n) < 5*mi_nbins ('bin-MI inactive; ranking deferred to rerank') or auto-shrink nbins to n//5.

title: Gate uses bootstrap LCB but ranking/top-K uses the point estimate
detail: With mi_gain_bootstrap_n>0 a spec is admitted on mi_gain_lcb yet competes for top_k_after_mi slots on spec.mi_gain: a high-variance candidate with a barely-passing LCB outranks a stable one with a better LCB - inconsistent with the gate's own noise-floor rationale (opt-in only; default bootstrap_n=0).
fix: When bootstrap is enabled, rank by (-mi_gain_lcb, name); document the choice either way.

title: Multi-base domain-violation fallback crashes: np.where broadcasts (n,) mask against (n,K) base
detail: For linear_residual_multi/geomean/pairwise specs, base_arr is (n,K) but domain_ok is (n,); np.where(domain_ok, base_arr, 1.0) raises ValueError whenever any base cell is non-finite. Reproduced: NaN in one base cell + NaN-tolerant inner (LGB/CB-style) -> 'operands could not be broadcast (200,) (200,2)'. The fallback machinery itself crashes in its target scenario.
fix: mask = domain_ok[:, None] if base_arr.ndim == 2 else domain_ok; base_safe = np.where(mask, base_arr, 1.0). Add regression test: multi-base wrapper + NaN base row at predict returns y_train_median fallback, no crash; verify test fails pre-fix.

title: Per-model wrap builds y-clip/median/t-clip from FULL y (train+val+test); promised end-of-target re-fit never runs
detail: emit_per_model_composite_y_scale_test passes y_train=_y_full_arr (full y) to from_fitted_inner; comment claims the end-of-target pass re-fits the envelope, but _run_composite_target_wrapping:286 skips already-wrapped entries, so y_clip_low/high, y_train_median and t_clip permanently embed test-row y statistics -> holdout contamination + contract violation (docstring: 'Training-row y values').
fix: In _run_composite_target_wrapping, when entry is already a CompositeTargetEstimator, recompute fitted_params_ y_clip/y_train_median/t_clip from _y_train_for_wrap (filtered_train_idx slice) instead of continue; or pass the train slice to the per-model hook. Regression: full-flow wrapper envelope == train-slice-only envelope.

title: predict_quantile crashes for every requires_base=False (unary) transform
detail: predict_quantile unconditionally extracts base columns; for unary transforms (cbrt_y/log_y/yeo_johnson_y/quantile_normal_y, all in the DEFAULT discovery list) base_column is unset, _resolve_base_columns() returns (), and _extract_base_matrix raises 'base_columns is empty; multi-base transforms require...'. Reproduced on cbrt_y. _predict_unclipped branches on requires_base; predict_quantile does not.
fix: Mirror _predict_unclipped: when not transform.requires_base, skip extraction and pass np.zeros_like(t_q) to the inverse (unary adapter ignores base). Add unary quantile round-trip regression test.

title: fit() materialises a full copy of X even when zero rows fail domain_check (the common case)
detail: X_valid = self._subset_rows(X, valid) runs unconditionally; with an all-True mask pandas .loc[mask].reset_index() copies every block, polars .filter gathers a new frame, ndarray bool-indexing copies. On the 100+GB frames this wrapper rides, peak RSS doubles for nothing - the exact RAM failure mode CLAUDE.md bans.
fix: X_valid = X if n_invalid == 0 else self._subset_rows(X, valid). Behaviour identical (t_train/sample_weight are ndarrays; inner ignores index). Test with recording stub asserting the same object reaches the inner when all rows valid.

title: PrePipelinePredictShim._transform swallows ALL exceptions and silently feeds untransformed X to the inner
detail: except Exception: return X. Scaler/imputer output is shape-compatible with raw X, so a linear/MLP inner fitted on scaled features predicts garbage on raw X with NO error when transform fails (e.g. sklearn feature-name/order mismatch at predict). Silently wrong component predictions inside the NNLS stack / OOF refit. Same pattern duplicated in ensemble/__init__.py:48-56.
fix: Stop swallowing: log WARNING + re-raise; if one pd/pl boundary case must be tolerated, catch only that exception type and assert output width matches before fallback. Regression: scaler fitted on named columns + reordered-column predict must not silently pass raw X.

title: predict_quantile broken for grouped transforms: no groups kwarg to inverse, group column not dropped for inner
detail: _predict_unclipped threads groups into transform.inverse and strips group_column before the inner call (lines 64-86); predict_quantile does neither. linear_residual_grouped inverse raises 'groups kwarg is required' (transforms/linear.py:541-544) and the string group column reaches the inner. Related: from_fitted_inner (_estimator.py:201) has no group_column param, so grouped specs cannot be post-hoc wrapped.
fix: Replicate the grouped plumbing in predict_quantile (extract groups -> inverse_kwargs; _drop_columns before inner.predict_quantile); add group_column parameter to from_fitted_inner. Regression test for grouped quantile round-trip.

title: try/except TypeError sample_weight fallback misattributes unrelated TypeErrors (3 sites)
detail: Sites: _estimator.py:494-504 (transform.fit), _estimator.py:569-578 (inner fit), post_shim.py:52-58. A TypeError raised INSIDE a weight-accepting fit is misdiagnosed as 'does not accept sample_weight' and silently retried unweighted -> silent behaviour change (unweighted training) plus a log line asserting the wrong cause; also risks double partial fit.
fix: Gate on signature: sklearn.utils.validation.has_fit_parameter(est, 'sample_weight') for estimators, inspect.signature(transform.fit).parameters for transform functions (once per fit). Re-raise TypeError when the signature said weights are accepted.

title: feature_importances_/coef_/intercept_/booster_ return None when fitted inner lacks the attr (hasattr trap)
detail: getattr(inner, attr, None) makes hasattr(wrapper,'feature_importances_') True with value None on any fitted wrapper (reproduced). Downstream duck-typing (if hasattr: x.sum()) crashes on None far from the cause or records None importances. booster_ docstring claims NotFittedError but returns None post-fit; get_booster makes hasattr-based XGB detection always match.
fix: Post-fit, use plain getattr(est, attr) so missing attrs raise AttributeError (hasattr then correctly False; pre-fit NotFittedError already subclasses AttributeError). Keep n_features_in_ None-pre-fit convention. Update booster_ docstring.

title: predict/predict_quantile raise RuntimeError instead of NotFittedError before fit
detail: Reproduced: pre-fit predict raises RuntimeError (also predict_quantile at line 244-247), while _utils properties and update() use NotFittedError - inconsistent within the class and against sklearn convention. Callers catching NotFittedError miss it; RuntimeError is not an AttributeError subclass so it escapes hasattr-style probes.
fix: Raise sklearn.exceptions.NotFittedError at both sites (subclasses ValueError+AttributeError, so existing broad catches keep working).

title: from_fitted_inner T-clip envelope [-10*std(y), +10*std(y)] is centered at zero; mis-centers unary transforms
detail: Sound for residual transforms (T~0) but log_y/cbrt_y/yeo_johnson_y (default discovery list) have T centered at the transformed y level. When |center(T)| > 10*std(y) (near-constant large-offset target, e.g. y~1e5+-1 -> T~11.5, envelope +-10) ALL T_hat are clipped, the inverse collapses and y-clip flattens predictions to a constant. fit() path correctly centers at median(T) with MAD (lines 609-623).
fix: For requires_base=False transforms compute T_train exactly inside from_fitted_inner via transform.forward(y_train, zeros, params) and reuse the fit() MAD-centered logic; document the residual-centered assumption for the bivariate proxy. Add biz-value regression on log_y large-offset target.

title: Grouped transforms: feature_names_in_ includes group_column but n_features_in_ delegates to inner fitted without it
detail: fit captures feature_names_in_ from the ORIGINAL X (incl. group_column) while the inner is fitted on X minus group_column; n_features_in_ (property, _utils.py:53-66) delegates to the inner -> len(feature_names_in_) == F vs n_features_in_ == F-1, breaking the sklearn invariant relied on by introspection and mlframe predict-side column resolution.
fix: Store the wrapper's own n_features_in_ at fit (len(feature_names_in_)/X.shape[1]); delegate to the inner only as fallback when the wrapper attr is absent (from_fitted_inner route).

title: Grouped transforms: pandas X.drop(columns=[group_column]) copies the whole frame at fit AND every predict
detail: _drop_columns (lines 668-688, also _predict.py:82-86) uses X.drop; under pandas 2.x non-CoW (installed 2.3.3 default) this materialises the full remaining frame per call - on possibly 100+GB frames, per predict batch. Polars path is cheap (Arrow buffer reuse); cost is pandas-specific.
fix: Prefer CoW-lazy column subset (X[remaining_cols] with CoW enabled) or document/enable pandas CoW; hoist the surviving-column list. Measure before/after per the perf-measure-first rule.

title: Streaming refit updates alpha/beta but leaves y_clip/t_clip/y_train_median stale under the detected drift
detail: After a drift-triggered refit, predict still applies pre-drift t_clip (MAD of old T_train), pre-drift y envelope and old median fallback. Under a level/scale regime change - the event that fired the refit - corrected predictions land outside the stale y-envelope and get clipped back toward the old regime, undoing the correction.
fix: On info['refit'], also refresh y_clip_low/high via _y_train_clip_bounds(buffer y), y_train_median, and t-clip from buffer residuals under the new (alpha, beta); ship ON with the refit (corrective-mechanism default-on rule) and log the new bounds.

title: predict_quantile parity gaps vs predict: no domain fallback, no T-clip, no runtime_stats_/callback
detail: NaN base passes the np.any(base_arr<0) ratio guard (NaN<0 is False) -> silent NaN quantiles; ratio with base==0 silently yields 0. No T-scale clip (heavy-tail quantile-head blow-ups documented at _estimator.py:598-608 pass to the inverse). Quantile traffic invisible to runtime_stats_ and the monitoring callback that the point path feeds.
fix: Factor the domain-mask + fallback + T-clip + counter block out of _predict_unclipped and reuse it per quantile column in predict_quantile.

title: Missing fit-time validation: unary y/X length mismatch and fallback_predict typo surface late and cryptically
detail: len(y)==len(X) is checked only in the requires_base branch (lines 422-433); for unary transforms a mismatch surfaces as pandas 'Boolean index has wrong length' inside _subset_rows. fallback_predict is validated only on the first predict batch that contains a domain violation (_predict.py:135-143) - a typo can sit undetected until production.
fix: In fit(): always validate y length against the frame row count, and validate fallback_predict in ('y_train_median','nan') (sklearn convention: validate in fit).

title: _extract_base claims structured-ndarray support it does not have; dead ndarray fallback in _extract_base_matrix
detail: Docstring + TypeError message say 'or a structured ndarray with named columns' but every ndarray raises TypeError. _extract_base_matrix:189-191 fallback loop ('preserves prior behaviour for ndarray-with-names') delegates to _extract_base which raises - dead code. Misleading when debugging dropped-column/wrong-X errors. _subset_rows DOES support ndarray, so fit is inconsistent across helpers.
fix: Either implement the structured-ndarray path (X[col].astype(np.float64) when X.dtype.names contains it) or correct the docstrings/messages and delete the dead fallback.

title: T-clip hits not tracked in runtime_stats_/callback; WARNING logged on every affected batch
detail: t_low_hits/t_high_hits are computed, logged at WARNING per batch (a miscalibrated Huber inner spams one warning per predict call in prod) and then discarded - unlike y-clip and domain counters which are cumulative and shipped to the monitoring callback. runtime_stats_ docstring omits T-clip.
fix: Add t_clip_low_hits/t_clip_high_hits to runtime_stats_ and the callback payload; WARN once (or every Nth occurrence), DEBUG thereafter.

title: CompositeSpec frozen dataclass is unhashable and __eq__ crashes when fitted_params holds ndarrays
detail: frozen=True generates __hash__ over fields but fitted_params: dict is unhashable -> hash(spec) raises TypeError. Generated __eq__ compares the dicts; for median_residual (bin_edges/bin_medians ndarrays) or monotonic_residual knots, spec_a == spec_b raises 'truth value of an array is ambiguous'. Dict/Tuple imports unused.
fix: Declare fitted_params (and mi/diagnostic floats if desired) with field(compare=False); identity fields name/target_col/transform_name/base_column/extra_base_columns define eq/hash. Drop unused imports.

title: Shim: estimator_ property defeats check_is_fitted; predict_quantile not delegated
detail: The estimator_ property exists pre-fit (returns the unfitted inner) so sklearn check_is_fitted passes on an unfitted shim. The shim delegates only fit/predict: a quantile-capable CompositeTargetEstimator nested in a shim loses predict_quantile, so predict_quantile_ensemble rejects it ('lacks predict_quantile') though the member supports it.
fix: Delegate predict_quantile(X, alpha) through _transform when the inner exposes it; add __sklearn_is_fitted__ that reflects the inner's fitted state.

title: Dead imports in estimator/__init__.py and _estimator.py; shadowed logging import; pickling caveat undocumented
detail: __init__.py: warnings, deque, NotFittedError, BaseEstimator, RegressorMixin, clone, Callable, Dict, List unused (grep-verified); logger never used. _estimator.py: math/deque/NotFittedError unused; line 590 re-imports logging as _logging despite the module logger. runtime_stats_callback docstring omits that a lambda/closure callback makes the fitted wrapper unpicklable.
fix: Remove dead imports, use the module logger at _estimator.py:590, add a docstring note to pass a module-level callable for runtime_stats_callback when the model will be persisted.

title: Compliance matrix covers only diff + pandas + LinearRegression for CompositeTargetEstimator
detail: No rows for unary (requires_base=False), multi-base, grouped, polars X, predict_quantile, or predict-time domain-violation fallback - E1/E3/E6 are crashes in exactly those uncovered configurations. (Pickle round-trip is NOT a gap: tests/training/test_composite.py:524 covers it.)
fix: Parametrize the compliance class over (diff/pandas, cbrt_y/no-base, linear_residual_multi/two-base, linear_residual_grouped/group-col, diff/polars) plus a domain-violation-fallback row; assert get_params/clone/fit/predict per row.

title: update() boxes the whole rolling buffer to Python floats and rebuilds two ndarrays on every call
detail: extend(y_arr.tolist()) boxes to PyFloat; np.asarray(deque) walks the full 10k-element buffer per update even when the z-check short-circuits on buffer_too_small. O(buffer) (~0.5-1ms at 10k) per micro-batch for a streaming caller.
fix: Use a preallocated float64 ring buffer (two ndarrays + head index) passing slice views to streaming_alpha_check_and_refit; at minimum skip ndarray materialisation while buffer_n < online_refit_min_buffer_n.

title: update() rejects linear_residual_robust although its fitted-param shape {alpha, beta} is identical
detail: Streaming refit allowlist is ('linear_residual',) only. linear_residual_robust shares forward/inverse and the exact alpha/beta params (transforms/registry.py:286-301), so the closed-form refit applies verbatim; robust-variant users lose drift correction for no technical reason.
fix: Extend the allowlist to include linear_residual_robust and use _linear_residual_robust_fit on the buffer for that case; add a streaming-update test for the robust variant.

title: predict_quantile_ensemble batched-call probe catches only TypeError/ValueError
detail: The one-shot batched predict_quantile(X, [alphas]) probe falls back to per-alpha calls only on (TypeError, ValueError). A member whose inner raises a library-specific error on sequence alpha (LightGBMError/CatBoostError surfaced through CompositeTargetEstimator) crashes the whole ensemble instead of using the documented scalar-alpha fallback.
fix: Catch Exception for the probe call only (DEBUG-log member index + exception), keeping strict propagation for the per-alpha scalar calls that form the real contract.

title: Yeo-Johnson inverse produces NaN outside lambda-dependent asymptote
detail: YJ inverse needs t*lam+1>0 (lam<0) / 1+t*(lam-2)>0 (lam>2); no clamp -> np.power(neg, frac)=NaN. Verified: lognormal target fits lam=2.1994, inverse of t_hat=-5.11 -> NaN; np.clip(nan)=nan so y-clip can't save it; wrapper T-clip (med+/-10MAD expanded to obs min/max) can cross the asymptote and t_mad==0 disables it; screening inverse has no T-clip. yeo_johnson_y + both YJ chains are in DEFAULT discovery list.
fix: Clamp power bases in _yj_inverse_numpy/_yj_inverse_numba_kernel/_yj_inverse_scalar: np.maximum(t*lam+1.0, 1e-12) and np.maximum(-t*(2-lam)+1.0, 1e-12) (logratio-style soft-cap). Regression test: lam<0 and lam>2 inverse on out-of-envelope t_hat must be finite.

title: linear_residual_multi condition gate scale-variant: mixed-unit independent bases falsely collinear
detail: BKW kappa<30 requires column-equilibrated matrix; code takes SVD cond of centered-but-UNSCALED columns. Verified: independent b1~N(0,1), b2~N(0,1e6) -> cond=1.0e6, collinear_fallback=True, alphas=[0,0] -> transform silently degenerates to T=y-mean(y). No caller checks collinear_fallback; default-ON multi-base auto-promotion (_fit.py:738) hits this directly.
fix: Normalise centered columns to unit L2 norm before SVD (base_centered/col_norms) = BKW scaled condition index, unit-invariant. biz_value test: mixed-scale independent bases must NOT fall back; near-duplicate columns must.

title: smoothing_spline_residual s has wrong units: m*std(signal) instead of m*var(noise) -> oversmoothing
detail: scipy UnivariateSpline s targets residual SS; correct scale is m*sigma2_noise. Code uses m*std(yc_avg): wrong units AND wrong quantity. Verified: y=5*sin(b)+N(0,0.1), n=3000 -> s=9990 (proper ~30), residual std 1.825 vs 0.1 noise floor, only 70% of variance absorbed (99.96% absorbable). Transform fails its stated purpose; discovery comparisons systematically pessimistic.
fix: Estimate noise via Rice first-difference on b-sorted y: sigma2=sum(diff(y)^2)/(2(m-1)); set s=m*sigma2. Pass w=sqrt(count) per duplicate-averaged knot. biz_value test: sine target residual std <= 2x noise floor.

title: ratio inverse uses raw base while forward divides by eps-floored base: round-trip broken in-domain
detail: _ratio_domain admits any |base|>0; forward divides by safe_base (eps floor), inverse multiplies by RAW base. Verified: base=1e-9, eps=9.27e-7 -> round-trip rel err 99.9% on a valid row. Also manufactures huge T outliers (y/eps) that poison the T-MAD clip envelope.
fix: Apply the identical safe_base floor in _ratio_inverse (round-trip exact by construction); optionally count |base|<eps rows in fit-time stats.

title: centered_ratio inverse: eps asymmetry + sign-flipped predictions when base drops below train envelope
detail: Inverse multiplies by raw (base+c) while forward floors it (round-trip break, same class as T4). Worse: c fitted so min(train base)+c=+0.01*scale; predict base below that makes (base+c)<0 and flips prediction sign. Verified: train base in [5,10], c=-4.928, t_hat=1.5 -> +1.61 at base=6 but -1.39 at base=4. Domain check passes such rows (finiteness only).
fix: Floor (base+c) with the same signed-eps rule in the inverse; treat base+c<=0 as predict-time domain violation once a params-aware domain hook exists (T15), or at least count into runtime_stats_.

title: rolling_quantile_ratio inverse multiplies by raw rolling median, forward by eps-floored one
detail: Third instance of T4 asymmetry class: forward uses safe=where(|roll_med|<eps, sign*eps, roll_med), inverse uses roll_med directly. Rows with near-zero rolling median round-trip with error factor roll_med/eps; sign flip possible at exactly 0.
fix: Reuse the same signed-eps floor line in _rolling_quantile_ratio_inverse.

title: James-Stein sigma2 proxy ignores per-group base variance; shrinkage factor unit-dependent
detail: OLS slope variance is sigma2/(n_g*Var(base_g)); code uses sigma2_total/mean(n_g), dropping Var(base) and collapsing group sizes. Shrinkage wrong by factor Var(base): over-shrinks when Var(base)>1. Verified: base~N(10,2) (Var=4) -> 4x overestimated noise -> c=1.0 full collapse to global alpha.
fix: Accumulate per-group Var(base_g) in the OLS loop (linear.py:462-488) and use mean over groups of sigma2/(n_g*Var(base_g)) as the JS noise proxy.

title: Grouped shrinkage rescales alpha_g but never re-centres beta_g: systematic per-group offset in T
detail: beta_g was fitted jointly with unshrunk alpha_g; replacing alpha while keeping beta makes per-group residual mean = (a_g - a_shrunk)*mean(base_g) != 0. Verified: 8 groups x 60 rows, noise sd 2 (SE~0.26), c=1.0 -> per-group T means -1.88..+1.90, far beyond noise. Defeats the residualisation the per-group OLS performed.
fix: Store mean(base_g) during the per-group pass; in the shrink loop set per_group_betas[g] += (a_g - a_shrunk)*mean(base_g). Test: per-group T means ~= 0 after shrinkage fires.

title: monotonic_residual degenerate early-returns omit is_degenerate; most-degenerate fits bypass discovery rejection
detail: Full-fit path stamps is_degenerate/var_explained, consumed at discovery/_eval.py:66 to reject T~=y-const specs. Both early returns (nonlinear.py:426-434, 442-450; too few rows / knots collapsed e.g. constant base) return constant-g params WITHOUT the flag. Verified: fit on constant base returns no is_degenerate key -> spec proceeds to full training.
fix: Add "is_degenerate": True, "var_explained": 0.0 to both early-return dicts.

title: Auto-knot rule n_unique//200 conflates cardinality with discreteness: continuous bases on n<=2400 get 3-5 knots
detail: For continuous base n_unique~=n: n=600 -> 3 knots, n=1000 -> 5, full 12 only at n>=2400 - though min_knot_n=30 already guards small slabs (12 knots at n=1000 = ~83 rows/slab, healthy). Systematic underfit of sigmoid/saturating shapes at exactly the tiny-rerank sample sizes; degrades the transform and discovery's opinion of it.
fix: Cap only when base is genuinely low-cardinality vs n (e.g. if n_unique < 4*n_knots: cap to max(3, n_unique//2)), or use divisor ~50 so min_knot_n stays the binding constraint.

title: _rolling_median bottleneck path diverges from pandas fallback (every row for even k; last k//2 rows for odd k)
detail: Verified by emulating move_median: even k=4 off-by-one window alignment -> 19/20 positions differ from pandas center=True; odd k=7 tail filled with constant last-full-window median vs pandas truncated re-centred medians ([16,16,16] vs [16.5,17,17.5]). Docstring's 'consistent with min_periods=1 semantics' is false. T values depend on whether bottleneck is installed -> non-reproducible targets across envs.
fix: Even k: shift to match pandas convention (use _fwd[i+(k-1)//2]). Tail: compute true truncated centred medians for last k//2 rows (negligible cost). Add bottleneck-vs-pandas parity test for even+odd k incl. boundaries.

title: rolling_quantile_ratio centred window reads future base rows: look-ahead in time-ordered use
detail: T_i = y_i / RollingMedian_k(base)[i] with centred window uses base[i+1..i+k//2] (future rows) for both train-target construction and inverse. Silent look-ahead in backtests; streaming inference cannot compute the inverse for the newest k//2 rows. EWMA is correctly causal by contrast. Config excludes it from defaults for ordering reasons only; no warning about future-window dependence for explicit users.
fix: Add mode='trailing' (median over [i-k+1, i]) as the time-series default; keep centred as opt-in with explicit look-ahead warning in docstring + registry description.

title: Domain-filtering compacts row sequence before time-recurrent forwards (ewma/rolling/frac_diff)
detail: Wrapper drops invalid rows then runs recurrent forward on the COMPACTED sequence: EWMA state / rolling window / frac-diff lags at rows after a gap differ from the full-frame predict-time computation (where kernels carry state through non-finite rows). Fitted mapping near gaps is trained on values that cannot occur in deployment. EWMA kernel already supports NaN carry-forward, making the row-drop unnecessary.
fix: Tag sequence transforms (TAG_SEQUENTIAL); compute forward on full-length arrays with NaN-aware kernels and apply the valid mask only afterwards to select training rows; never re-index before the recurrence.

title: frac_diff is y-only but registered requires_base=True: duplicate identical specs per base + needless row drops
detail: forward/inverse ignore base entirely (nonlinear.py:864-891) yet registry defaults requires_base=True and domain demands finite base (nonlinear.py:892-900). Discovery dedup (_fit.py:409) applies only to requires_base=False, so configuring frac_diff evaluates one identical candidate per base with misleading distinct names (y-fdiff-lag1/lag2...); base NaNs also drop rows, compacting the y sequence (compounds T13). Config-opt-in (not in defaults).
fix: Set requires_base=False on the frac_diff registry entry; make _frac_diff_domain ignore base when y is not None (mirror y_quantile_clip).

title: Params-dependent domains unenforceable: log_y forward NaNs on screen/val rows below -offset; 2-arg log_y_domain is dead code
detail: log_y true domain is y > -offset, known only post-fit. Adapter wraps lambda y: log_y_domain(y) (drops params); discovery domain gate runs before fit. Verified: offset=1.0, log_y_forward([-2.0])=[nan] -> NaN T flows into MI screening silently for val/screen rows below train min - 1. Same structural gap leaves T5's base+c<=0 rows 'valid'.
fix: Add optional domain_check_fitted(y, base, params) to the Transform contract (default = params-less check); call after fit in discovery screening/eval; wire log_y's existing 2-arg domain and a shifted>0 check for centered_ratio into it.

title: YJ numba forward 'bit-identical' docstring claim is false (fastmath=True)
detail: Verified max |numba-numpy| up to 1.14e-13 (lam=-1.3, n=50k) - within the project ~1e-9 band so the dispatch is fine, but the bit-identity claim is wrong (the inverse kernel's own comment at :69-71 explains why fastmath breaks bit-exactness).
fix: Correct docstring to 'equal within ~1e-13'; pin a tolerance regression test comparing numba vs numpy paths across lambda branches.

title: Dead duplicated numba kernels + MAD constants across three modules
detail: _ewma_kernel/_frac_diff_inverse_kernel duplicated identically in transforms/__init__.py:31-58 and linear.py:46-78 (both unused; nonlinear.py owns the live ones). _MAD_FLOOR_FRAC/_MAD_SOFT_CAP_K duplicated in linear.py:87-90 + nonlinear.py:133-136 but dead (_logratio_fit lazy-imports the parent's copies). Edit-site drift hazard.
fix: Delete dead copies; single definitions in nonlinear.py (kernels) and __init__.py (constants); also dedupe the doubled logger lines linear.py:28/94.

title: _finite_mask kwarg on 8 fit functions documented as dispatcher-fed but never passed by any caller
detail: simple.py:87,170,249,290,341 + nonlinear.py:287,402,560,851 take _finite_mask 'from the outer Transform.fit dispatcher' - grep shows zero callers pass it (discovery/_eval.py:56 calls fit plain). Advertised shared-isfinite optimisation never realised; comments describe nonexistent infrastructure.
fix: Thread the mask from eval_one_transform (it already computed valid) to realise the win, or delete the dead kwarg + comments.

title: Transform contract claims params dict 'is JSON-serialisable'; six transforms return ndarrays
detail: median_residual, quantile_residual, monotonic_residual, rank_residual, smoothing_spline_residual, quantile_normal_y all store ndarrays in fitted params; frac_diff deliberately .tolist()s its weights for exactly this reason. Naive json.dumps(spec.fitted_params) crashes; convention inconsistent.
fix: Either amend the contract docstring (ndarray values allowed, use spec serialiser) or .tolist() the small arrays (all O(n_bins/knots), cheap) for uniformity with frac_diff.

title: is_composite_target_name substring heuristic false-positives on plausible user columns
detail: Fragments include common tokens (-diff-, -ratio-, -spline-, -interact-): user columns like price-diff-7d or debt-ratio-q are classified as composite targets and get MTRESID metric labelling.
fix: Parse strictly: require {target}-{alias}-{base} with alias as the second dash-separated token (and known target prefix where available) instead of any-substring matching.

title: Unary specs named with a spurious base segment (y-cbrtY-lag1 though lag1 is ignored)
detail: compose_target_name(target, transform, base) is called unconditionally; unary work items carry the current loop base (_fit.py:397-413), so the dedup'd single unary spec gets named after whichever base candidate came first - misleading in reports and unstable across base orderings.
fix: For requires_base=False compose with a sentinel base ('none' or empty -> 2-segment form y-cbrtY) and teach is_composite_target_name the unary form.

title: Silent 'except Exception: pass' around pandas-groupby fast paths can mask real bugs
detail: _median_residual_per_bin_medians (simple.py:161-166) and _quantile_residual_per_bin_stats (nonlinear.py:271-277) silently reroute to the v1 loop on ANY exception - a genuine v2-path defect (pandas API change, dtype regression) would hide forever; the project's tracked silent-error-swallowing class.
fix: logger.warning once with the exception before falling back to v1.

title: logratio soft-cap makes inverse inexact for in-domain rows beyond 10 MAD; deliberate but unpinned
detail: Rows with |T-median| > 10*MAD (~6.7 sigma; reachable on heavy-tail logratio) don't round-trip - a sound safety tradeoff, but neither the registry description nor any test pins the capped branch.
fix: Document the round-trip consequence in the registry description; add a two-sided test (capped row inverts to the cap bound; in-cap row round-trips to 1e-12).

title: EWMA inverse cold-starts every predict batch from the train-mean anchor
detail: Stateless params mean each predict batch recomputes EWMA from anchor=mean(base_train): for the canonical test-follows-train deployment the first ~k rows use the train global mean instead of the actual recent level, biasing y_hat where recency matters most. frac_diff shares the property but is forward/inverse self-consistent.
fix: Store the final train EWMA state in params and add warm_start=True (use stored state when the predict frame chronologically continues train), or accept an optional base_history kwarg.

title: No adversarial round-trip/parity tests: every verified bug class above lacked coverage
detail: Existing round-trips use benign data only. Missing: eps-edge round-trips (ratio/centered_ratio/rolling_quantile_ratio), YJ inverse beyond lambda-asymptote (lam<0, lam>2), bottleneck-vs-pandas rolling parity (even+odd k, boundaries), per-group T means ~=0 after JS shrinkage, linear_residual_multi non-fallback on mixed-scale independent bases, ewma/frac_diff fit-vs-predict T equality with scattered NaN rows.
fix: Add the six <=20-line synthetic tests enumerated in the findings file; each pins one verified failure mode from T1/T4/T5/T6/T8/T11/T13.

title: sample_weight misaligned with fit rows after group-aware eval carve
detail: _carve_inner_eval_split's group branch returns mask-SCATTERED fit rows (line 285) but all five call sites slice weights as a prefix sw[:len(y_fit)] (431-434, 463-465, 703, 868-871, 902-904). With group_ids+sample_weight (both threaded by the suite), every inner OOF refit trains with weights attached to the wrong rows. Silent: lengths match, no error.
fix: Make _carve_inner_eval_split accept sample_weight and return carved sw_fit (or return fit-row indices/mask) and use it at all five call sites. Regression test: non-uniform weights + group carve, assert estimator received sw[fit_mask].

title: kfold OOF composite branch has no eval-set carve; ES boosters silently dropped
detail: kfold>1 composite path fits inner clones with no eval_set (670) while the raw path (698-707), single-split (863-875) and external (422-438) branches all carve one. Inner clones with early-stopping callbacks raise and are excluded per-component (713-719) - the exact prod pathology in _maybe_pass_sample_weight's docstring. Suite DEFAULT is oof_holdout_source='kfold'.
fix: Mirror the single-split composite path: carve (X_fit,t_fit,X_ev,t_ev) from (X_stack_valid,t_stack) with fold-subset group ids, pass eval_set=. Test: kfold + composite spec + ES inner must survive.

title: Outer OOF split never group-aware: same-group rows span refit-train and holdout
detail: group_ids only drives the inner ES carve (691-697, 818-823); the outer split is plain shuffled KFold (618-621) or rng.permutation (791-794). On grouped data, within-group memorisation inflates every component's OOF quality, so NNLS/linear-stack weights and the dummy-floor gate run on a leaked surface - the inner split is defended against exactly this, the outer isn't.
fix: When group_ids is not None use GroupKFold in the kfold path and a group-level permutation carve in the single-split path (permute unique groups, accumulate to n_holdout).

title: Explicit non-monotone time_ordering silently downgrades to random shuffle
detail: use_time_split only flips when time_ordering is already monotone (777-783); non-monotone (panel frames sorted by group-then-time, or any NaT->NaN at 178) silently becomes a random shuffle with no log - future leaks into refit-train on AR targets, OOF optimistic, weights/gates biased. Suite threads ctx.timestamps so reachable in prod. Tests pin only monotone/no-signal cases.
fix: Stable-argsort time_ordering and take the trailing holdout_frac of the SORTED order (past-train/future-holdout regardless of row order); minimum: WARN that the explicit time signal was ignored. Pin both behaviours.

title: composite_predictions_as_feature deep-copies the whole pandas frame
detail: out = df.copy() defaults deep=True - full duplication of every column. CLAUDE.md RAM rule (CRITICAL) bans df.copy() on hot paths: frames reach 100+GB, copy doubles peak RAM -> OOM. This helper is the documented composite-x-FE stacking building block on the training frame. Polars branch (71) is fine; pandas pays the copy.
fix: Size-gate per repo convention: <~2GB keep the copy; above, add the column in place with a documented mutation contract (inplace knob + try/finally removal, the mutate-and-restore pattern).

title: OOF refit reuses transform fitted_params estimated on full train incl. holdout
detail: kfold (659-660) and single-split (849-851) branches use spec['fitted_params'] (e.g. linear_residual alpha fit on FULL train) to build fold T and to predict the holdout - holdout y values influenced the params used to predict that holdout. Systematic optimism for composite vs raw components, tilting ensemble weights. Transform.fit exists (transforms/__init__.py:124) and is OLS-cheap. External branch unaffected.
fix: Refit transform params per fold: transform.fit(y_stack[valid], base_stack[valid]); use fold params in forward and from_fitted_inner; keep spec params as fallback on refit failure.

title: OOF memo cache key omits holdout_frac, time mode, external-holdout identity, group_ids
detail: _full_key=(cache_key,kfold,random_state). Same caller key with different holdout_frac, time_ordering (random vs trailing slice), external_holdout presence, or group_ids returns the first call's matrix for the second request; external-holdout results share the key with train-tail results (511-512).
fix: Extend the key: round(holdout_frac,6), time-mode flag, external flag/len, cheap groups fingerprint (len + sha1 of first/last 64 ids) - RAM-rule safe.

title: kfold>1 silently overrides external holdout, contradicting documented precedence
detail: Docstring (547-549) says external holdout is preferred when supplied; the kfold>1 branch returns (616-745) before the external check (752), so a caller passing both gets K-fold with the external frame ignored and no log. Suite never sets both, but the function is public API used directly by 6+ tests/benchmarks.
fix: Hoist the external-holdout check above the kfold branch to match the docstring (or WARN + document actual precedence); pin with a test.

title: external_holdout_base_per_spec accepted and documented as required - never read
detail: _compute_oof_with_external_holdout declares the param but the body never references it (grep-verified). Docstring (553-557) tells callers they're responsible for supplying it; the suite builds _base_val_per_spec to satisfy a dead contract. The param exists for a real failure mode (shim drops base column from X_holdout_t -> predict fails -> silent component drop).
fix: Wire it as the fallback base source when the column is missing from X_holdout_t (converts silent drops into predictions), or delete param + doc sentence + caller-side construction.

title: _maybe_pass_sample_weight silently retries without sw/eval_set on broad ValueError
detail: except (TypeError, ValueError): pass around the weighted fit, then unweighted retries. If the weighted call fails for a DATA reason (sklearn signals most data problems as ValueError) and the plain call succeeds, the model trains UNWEIGHTED with zero logging - silent error swallowing; also up to 3 fit attempts before a genuine error surfaces.
fix: Validate len(sw)==len(y) up front (raise); WARN naming dropped kwargs on each fallback hop; keep broad catch only for signature probing, narrow fit-retry trigger to TypeError.

title: _transform_via swallows pre-pipeline failures: OOF evaluates a non-deployed pipeline
detail: except Exception: return X with no log. If a fitted shim pre_pipeline.transform raises, the inner is refit on RAW X while the deployed shim predicts through transformed space - OOF RMSE measures a model the suite never deploys, weights assigned to the wrong object. If only one of the stack/holdout calls fails, train/predict spaces silently diverge.
fix: WARN with component name + exception on every fallback; treat one-side-only transform success as a component failure (visible drop) instead of proceeding.

title: detect_gpu_in_use: XGBoost USE_CUDA build flag != GPU in use; LightGBM dead import
detail: xgb.build_info()['USE_CUDA'] reports build capability and stock PyPI wheels ship CUDA-enabled - CPU-only hosts get flagged 'xgboost GPU in use' and the suite emits a misleading non-determinism warning. The lightgbm branch (111-118) imports the lib (~0.5s cold) and never appends anything - pure cost, no effect.
fix: AND the build flag with a physical-device probe (pyutilz.system.gpu_dispatch.is_cuda_available per repo convention); delete the no-op lightgbm import, keep the comment.

title: env_signature cold-imports 9 libraries to read versions - on the predict path
detail: __import__(lib) fully imports catboost/lightgbm/xgboost (~0.5-3s each). predict.py:106-110 calls env_signature() on model load, so cold inference pays seconds for version strings - against the file's own ENS-Low-7 keep-imports-off-predict rationale. Also missing python + mlframe versions, which matter for pickle drift.
fix: Use importlib.metadata.version(dist) with a name map (sklearn->scikit-learn), PackageNotFoundError->None; add platform.python_version() and mlframe version.

title: Train-matrix stash is dead in production; three docstrings describe removed refit path
detail: predict is NO-REFIT (528-536) and nothing reads _linear/_nnls_stack_train_preds/_train_y (grep: only a presence assert in test_ensembling_votenrank_votenrank.py:254). Factories still stash 8*n*(K+1) bytes RAM (~720MB at n=10M,K=8); cap_inference_components comments self-contradict (623-625 vs 633-639); discard_train_matrix docstring claims a predict-time refit path and a .refit method that don't exist.
fix: Stop stashing by default (opt-in keep_train_matrix for diagnostics), update the three docstring/comment sites to NO-REFIT reality, re-frame the votenrank test.

title: stacking_aware_gate thresholds RAW NNLS weights, documented as share of unit budget
detail: Survivors filtered by raw_weights >= min_weight, but min_weight=0.05 is documented as '5% of the unit weight budget' (155). With biased/scaled components NNLS sums can be e.g. 1.3, silently shifting the effective threshold to ~3.8% - gate semantics drift with input calibration.
fix: Threshold on w/w.sum() (guard sum>0, else existing degenerate path); the survivor renormalisation block (192-202) already exists.

title: residual_dedup_indices never validates len(oof_rmses) == K
detail: keep_pref iterates len(oof_rmses) candidates against a KxK corr. Shorter rmse vector: columns [len..K-1] never enter kept OR dropped - keep-list silently discards valid members (suite then drops those components at _phase_composite_post_xt_ensemble:628-631). Longer: IndexError deep in the loop. Sibling entry points all length-validate.
fix: raise ValueError when oof_rmses.shape[0] != K.

title: from_train_metrics mixes train-scale baseline with OOF RMSEs; notes/strategy mislabeled
detail: With component_oof_rmse given but baseline_oof_rmse=None, a caller's baseline_train_rmse is compared to OOF RMSEs (train RMSE is systematically lower) -> spurious 'no component beats baseline' fallbacks / diluted weights. notes keys always say *train* (472-474) even for OOF values; strategy is always 'oof_weighted' (471) even on the train fallback.
fix: Ignore baseline_train_rmse with WARN when OOF rmses supplied without OOF baseline (or require matching scale); record notes['rmse_source']='oof'|'train' and name keys accordingly.

title: _carve_inner_eval_split falls back to group-splitting tail carve when group path can't carve
detail: uniq.size<4 (267) or a dominant first group overshooting n-100 (281) silently falls through to the group-blind tail carve - splitting groups across fit/eval, the exact within-group-leak/under-stopping pathology the function's own docstring (244-251) cites as a prod incident. 2-3 large groups is a realistic panel shape.
fix: When group_ids present and the group carve can't be honoured, return (X,y,None,None) (ES degrades gracefully) or carve the smallest whole group as eval; never silently group-blind-split. Log it.

title: composite_oof_predictions: no groups/y to splitter, no per-fold sample_weight; prod call site never threads time
detail: kf.split(indices) passes neither y nor groups: cv_splitter=GroupKFold raises - group-honest OOF features impossible. fit_kwargs forwarded verbatim per fold (151): sample_weight there is length-mismatched/mis-weighted. Sole prod call site (discovery/_stacked.py:95,237) passes neither time_aware nor cv_splitter: shuffled folds leak future into the _oof_* stack features on temporal targets.
fix: Add groups=/sample_weight= params: kf.split(indices, groups=groups), slice sample_weight[train_idx] per fold; thread the discovery time signal + group ids at _stacked.py call sites.

title: OOF helper returns no holdout indices: sample-weighted stacking unreachable from suite
detail: from_linear_stack/from_nnls_stack have sample_weight support (_cross_target.py:139,269) but compute_oof_holdout_predictions returns only (matrix, y_holdout, names); holdout indices are discarded (kfold also drops NaN rows, 737-741), so _sw_for_oof cannot be aligned. Stack solvers run unweighted on weighted-objective targets (call site passes none, _phase...:707-720).
fix: Return a 4th element holdout_idx (indices into caller train order; kfold: finite-row-filtered natural order). Thread sample_weight[holdout_idx] into the stack solvers and optionally the OOF-RMSE gates.

title: No time-respecting K-fold OOF; default suite path deletes the time signal
detail: kfold+time downgrades to one trailing slice (609-615) and the suite default 'kfold' source nulls _time_ordering (caller 348-349) - so temporal targets get SHUFFLED K-fold OOF, each fold trained on future rows: the optimism the external-holdout path defends against. Forward-chaining folds (TimeSeriesSplit semantics, optional embargo a la purged CV) fit the existing per-fold refit loop; NaN-row dropping already tolerates uncovered head rows.
fix: Implement kfold>1 + monotone time as expanding-window forward-chaining folds; flip the suite to keep the time signal. biz_value test: AR(1) synthetic, forward-chained OOF RMSE honestly above shuffled by a pinned margin.

title: Inner eval carve seed hardcoded 0 in 4 of 5 sites, ignoring master seed
detail: Lines 424, 456, 864, 898 pass random_state=0 to _carve_inner_eval_split; kfold path (699) threads int(random_state). Contrary to derive_seeds sub-seed philosophy (73-93); changing oof_random_state never re-randomises the group eval carve - partially frozen randomness confusing seed-sweep variance estimates.
fix: Thread int(random_state) (or derive_seeds(random_state, ['inner_eval_carve'])) at all five sites.

title: Dead imports and dead locals across the package
detail: Unused: math (init:8), warnings (init:9), BaseEstimator/RegressorMixin (init:17), ElasticNetCV (init:23, _cross_target:21), in-function train_test_split never called (init:582), hashlib/warnings in _cross_target (10,13). Dead locals: fold_frac (init:622); order in stacking.py:121 whose companion comment (120) describes a worst-first strategy the code does not use.
fix: Delete the dead imports/locals; rewrite stacking.py:120 comment to match the best-first algorithm actually implemented.

title: residual_correlation_matrix: RuntimeWarning noise on constant columns; diagonal docstring wrong
detail: np.corrcoef on a zero-variance residual emits RuntimeWarning; sibling residual_dedup_indices guards the identical call with np.errstate(invalid='ignore') (118-119), this one doesn't. Docstring line 40 claims 'Diagonal is 1.0' - NaN for constant columns.
fix: Add the errstate guard; reword docstring: diagonal 1.0 for non-degenerate columns, NaN row/col indicates constant residual.

title: stacking.py degenerate fallbacks completely silent (module has no logger)
detail: Too-few-finite-rows (178-181) and NNLS-failure (185-189) paths return ALL names as survivors with uniform weights - the gate silently disables itself. Caller's INFO only fires when survivors < total, so a permanently degenerate gate is indistinguishable from 'gate kept everything'.
fix: Add module logger and WARN in both fallbacks with the reason + finite-row counts.

title: Stale from_linear_stack docstring; export_metadata omits is_convex
detail: Docstring claims intercept folded 'as an extra +b/n per component' and 'predict re-normalises only the magnitudes' - both removed: intercept stored+added (253, 540), non-convex predict never renormalises (525-550). export_metadata (561-568) lacks is_convex, so consumers can't tell convex average from raw additive solver weights; intercept only via notes.
fix: Rewrite the two docstring sentences; add 'is_convex' (and top-level 'intercept' for linear_stack) to export_metadata.

title: group_ids length checks inconsistent: >= max(idx)+1 accepts misaligned arrays
detail: kfold (694) and single-split (821) accept any group_ids at least max-index long, silently slicing a longer misaligned vector (full-frame groups passed where train-subset expected ruins the carve while looking valid). External path (418) and the carve (266) use exact == checks.
fix: Standardise to _g_arr.shape[0] == n_train at all four sites; WARN instead of the silent None fallback that hides caller bugs.

title: No coverage for highest-risk OOF parameter combinations
detail: Grep-verified: no test calls compute_oof_holdout_predictions with group_ids= (misses N1/N3); all kfold tests use component_specs=[None] so the kfold composite branch (N2) has zero coverage; non-monotone explicit time_ordering (N4) unpinned (test_composite_oof_time_aware_logged.py covers only monotone/no-signal); no test of cache hits across differing holdout_frac (N7).
fix: Add four targeted regression tests alongside the fixes (group+sw alignment, kfold composite+ES survival, non-monotone time signal, cache-key discrimination), each verified to fail on pre-fix code.

title: Rerank parallelism leaves inner LightGBM n_jobs=-1 uncapped (CPU oversubscription)
detail: _build_tiny_model sets LGBM n_jobs=-1; the n_jobs=1 cap in _one_fold only fires when fold-level tiny_model_n_jobs>1 (default 1). Default tiny_rerank_n_jobs=0 (auto) runs min(n_specs,cpu) specs in parallel threads, each spawning all-core LGBM fits: N x cpu threads. Same 4-8x wallclock pathology the in-fold warning describes, at a level with no cap.
fix: When _rerank_n_jobs>1, build tiny models with n_jobs=max(1, cpu//_rerank_n_jobs) by threading effective outer parallelism into _build_tiny_model/_tiny_cv_rmse_*, mirroring the fold-level cap.

title: Wilcoxon gate mathematically unpassable at default tiny_model_n_seed_repeats=3
detail: One-sided paired Wilcoxon with n=3 diffs has minimum exact p=1/8=0.125 > gate_alpha=0.05, so even a spec winning all 3 seeds is rejected; the len>=3 check admits exactly this case. Config default n_seed_repeats=3; comment recommends 5 but no validator enforces it. Enabling use_wilcoxon_gate with defaults silently rejects ALL specs.
fix: Add model_validator: if use_wilcoxon_gate and 0.5**n_seed_repeats > gate_alpha, raise or warn+skip the Wilcoxon stage. Unit test pinning the minimum-power math.

title: Multi-seed repeats are exact no-ops under TimeSeriesSplit/GroupKFold
detail: Multiseed wrappers vary only random_state; TSS/GroupKFold ignore it (identical folds every seed) and default families lightgbm(no subsampling)/ridge are seed-invariant, so all 3 repeats are bit-identical: 3x wasted Phase-B wall on every time-aware/grouped spec, and Wilcoxon per-seed arrays are pseudo-replicated (identical diffs treated as independent samples).
fix: Short-circuit to 1 repeat when the effective splitter is deterministic and the family seed-invariant; make the Wilcoxon gate require shuffled-KFold seed variation or fall back to the threshold gate with a log.

title: Raw baseline switches to TSS if ANY base is monotone; non-monotone specs gated against mismatched folds
detail: _any_base_monotone is one global flag: with one monotone base, the raw baseline CV is TimeSeriesSplit for ALL specs, but non-monotone specs were scored with shuffled KFold (optimistic). Threshold raw_baseline*tol comes from TSS (typically higher RMSE) -> gate too lenient for non-monotone specs; cross-spec ranking mixes two fold regimes. 'apples-to-apples' comment only holds when every base is monotone.
fix: Compute raw baseline per fold-scheme (one KFold, one TSS; cached, <=2x cost) and gate each spec against its matching scheme.

title: Composite tiny-CV scores only domain-valid rows; raw baseline and sibling specs score different row subsets
detail: _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale drops domain_check-failing rows (up to 30% at min_valid_domain_frac=0.7), usually the hard regime; raw baseline keeps all finite-y rows. Gate compares composite RMSE on an easier subset vs raw on all rows, and spec-vs-spec ranking (default config) compares different populations. Production predict covers ALL rows (median fallback), so deployed RMSE includes rows screening never scored.
fix: Fit on valid rows but score each val fold on ALL its rows, emulating the production median fallback for domain-invalid rows (the code already does this for non-finite inverse output at lines 686-693).

title: fit_stacked pass-2 specs based on _oof_* columns silently trained on all-NaN bases downstream
detail: Pass-2 specs whose base_column is an ephemeral _oof_<spec> column (the feature's headline output) merge into specs_ and reach suite training, where _build_full_column_from_splits (core/_misc_helpers.py:172) silently returns all-NaN for missing columns -> every row domain-invalid -> constant T -> garbage composite target trained with no error whenever use_stacked_discovery=True.
fix: Persist the OOF recipe on the spec and rebuild _oof_* columns via composite_oof_predictions before training; or make _build_full_column_from_splits raise/WARN on fully-missing columns and have fit_stacked drop ephemeral-base pass-2 specs with a log. Add an end-to-end regression test.

title: fit_stacked_on_residual pass-2 specs auto-trained against RAW y with residual-fitted params
detail: Docstring calls suite integration 'follow-up scaffolding', but _phase_composite_discovery.py:508 already wires it and line 587+ trains every spec in specs_ by applying forward(raw_y, base, params) with params fit on y - pass1_pred: never-validated targets trained silently. The discovered_on_residual marker (object.__setattr__, non-field on frozen dataclass) is read nowhere; names embed __y_residual__.
fix: Do not merge pass-2 residual specs into the suite-consumed specs_ (return on residual_specs_) until residual-aware training exists, or filter on the marker in the suite and promote it to a real CompositeSpec field. Integration test required.

title: fit_stacked rebuilds the full train-slice X_train inside the per-spec loop
detail: df.iloc[train_idx].reset_index(drop=True) (or polars mask+filter) executes once per spec in the top_specs loop -- up to 3 identical multi-GB materialisations of the train slice (OOM-grade on 100GB frames). Sibling fit_stacked_on_residual correctly hoists the identical slice out of its loop (lines 209-226).
fix: Move the X_train construction above the for-spec loop (verbatim hoist; loop body only needs the factory closure and composite_oof_predictions).

title: Forward stepwise has no finite-row masking: NaN-bearing lag bases disable or corrupt multi-base promotion
detail: No finite mask on y/bases and _linear_residual_multi_fit has none either. NaN seed base (lag features = canonical seed, leading NaNs): K=1 lstsq raises LinAlgError -> blanket except at _fit.py:725 skips the whole stepwise. NaN candidate: SVD raises in cond gate -> cond=inf -> collinear_fallback constant predictor -> candidate can never win. Multi-base promotion silently no-ops on autoregressive data.
fix: Build a joint finite mask over y + trial base columns inside _cv_rmse_with_folds (like Phase A's valid mask), and mask before the final _linear_residual_multi_fit at _fit.py:738. Regression test: seed with leading NaNs must still accept an orthogonal second base.

title: Silent fallback from GroupKFold to shuffled KFold when sample has < cv_folds groups
detail: if n_unique(groups) < cv_folds: groups=None reverts to shuffled KFold with no log (both raw at 256-258 and y-scale at 618-620), recreating the per-group-memoriser prod incident the group-aware path was added to fix whenever the 20k screening sample holds e.g. 2 groups with cv_folds=3. GroupKFold(n_splits=2) is strictly safer.
fix: Use GroupKFold(n_splits=min(cv_folds, n_groups)) when n_groups>=2; fall back to KFold only for n_groups<2, with a WARN.

title: Groups silently override time_aware: grouped time-series loses time ordering
detail: With both groups and time_aware=True, the splitter chain picks GroupKFold and drops the time-aware request without warning; GroupKFold mixes past/future within train folds, restoring the future->past leak on panel data. _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale also lacks the cv_splitter escape hatch its raw sibling has, so callers cannot inject a grouped-forward splitter.
fix: WARN when both are requested; better, add a grouped forward-chaining splitter (val groups time-later than train) and add cv_splitter to _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale for API symmetry.

title: early_stop_threshold (Pack #7, claimed 30-66% fold-compute saving) has zero callers
detail: Grep over src/mlframe: early_stop_threshold appears only inside _screening_tiny.py. No rerank/multiseed call passes it, so it is always inf and the serial early-stop never fires (and it only covers the n_jobs==1 branch). Same shipped-but-never-wired pattern as the mi_direct_gpu_batched incident.
fix: Wire early_stop_threshold=raw_baseline*tol into the rerank's multiseed calls when require_beats_raw_baseline is on, or remove the dead parameter with a bench-attempt note; add a test exercising the early-stop path.

title: Wilcoxon per-seed arrays drop failed seeds without identity: paired test can mis-pair
detail: Multiseed wrappers append only finite per-seed RMSEs (no seed index). If composite seed 2 and raw seed 3 fail, both arrays are length n-1, pass the len equality check at _tiny_rerank.py:639, and diff=comp-raw pairs the wrong seeds, invalidating the paired test exactly in fold-failure scenarios.
fix: Return fixed-length (n_seed_repeats) arrays with NaN for failed seeds; compute Wilcoxon diffs only on jointly-finite positions.

title: Per-bin raw baseline uses different folds (no time_aware) and single seed vs spec per-bin
detail: Regime gate compares spec per-bin (multiseed first pass, time_aware=base_t_aware, median over seeds) to raw per-bin from a single-seed _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y call that omits time_aware (global raw baseline at 468/488 does pass it). On monotone bases: spec=TSS vs raw=shuffled-KFold (optimistic) -> ratios biased high -> spurious regime rejections. Default-off (per_bin_n_bins=0).
fix: Pass per-base time_aware and use the same multiseed-median protocol for the raw per-bin as the first pass.

title: Per-bin fallback recompute omits time_aware AND groups
detail: The ENS-P2-5 fallback (recompute per-bin when missing from first-pass cache) calls _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale without time_aware and groups, so the recomputed breakdown uses shuffled KFold even when the first pass used TSS/GroupKFold -- cached and recomputed quantities are not the same estimator.
fix: Thread time_aware=_is_monotone_nondecreasing(base_screen) and groups=_groups_screen into the fallback call; both are in scope.

title: Time-aware base detection misses descending time-like bases and misfires on constants
detail: _is_monotone_nondecreasing: a monotone DECREASING time-like base (countdowns, depth-from-bottom logs) gets shuffled KFold -> future leak, while auto_detect explicitly recognises descending time columns (detector disagreement); a constant base passes (diff>=0) -> pointless TSS, wasting train rows and de-aligning folds vs sibling specs.
fix: Treat weakly-monotone non-constant in either direction as time-like (reverse rows for descending before TSS) and exclude constant arrays.

title: Stacked OOF predictions always shuffled KFold: time_aware exists but is never passed
detail: composite_oof_predictions has time_aware/cv_splitter params; fit_stacked (95-99) and fit_stacked_on_residual (237-241) pass only folds+seed -> shuffled KFold on temporal data. OOF pass-2 bases and the residual target are future-contaminated while sibling rerank/stepwise paths are time-aware (C-P2-11) -- inconsistent leakage discipline within one feature.
fix: Detect time-awareness like the rerank (monotone base / discovery time column) or add a time_aware kwarg (default True like stepwise) on both fit_stacked* methods and thread it through.

title: ranked[: max(1, len(ranked))] is a no-op slice: residual variant lacks a pass-1 spec cap
detail: fit_stacked caps OOF work at max_pass1_specs_to_stack=3; fit_stacked_on_residual computes OOF for EVERY pass-1 spec -- the dead max(1,len(ranked)) expression is a vestige of a missing cap parameter. Cost is linear in spec count and mean-aggregation over many weak specs dilutes the residual.
fix: Add max_pass1_specs_to_aggregate=3 mirroring the sibling, replace the no-op slice, expose via config alongside stacked_max_pass1_specs.

title: Forward stepwise CV is group-blind even when discovery is group-aware
detail: forward_stepwise_multi_base has no groups parameter and the _fit.py:714 call site does not pass _group_ids_for_rerank. On grouped data the marginal-gain CV mixes groups across train/val, so per-group-constant candidate bases show phantom gains -- the memoriser pathology the group-aware rerank fix documented. TSS helps only if rows happen to be group-sorted.
fix: Add a groups parameter with a GroupKFold path mirroring _tiny_cv_rmse_raw_y; pass the discovery group ids at _fit.py:714.

title: Per-call float64 pool copies and per-trial column_stack churn
detail: candidates={...float64...} re-materialises the whole pool per call: _fit.py caches float32 arrays, so K specs sharing one pool pay K full float64 conversions (~800MB transient each at the documented 4Mx25 shape). The greedy loop also rebuilds the trial matrix via column_stack for every candidate every round.
fix: Convert the pool to float64 once at the _fit.py cache level (or keep float32; the fit astype-promotes anyway) and reuse a preallocated (n, max_k) trial buffer in the candidate loop.

title: bayesian_alpha_fit_bootstrap(n_bootstrap=0) crashes in np.percentile on an empty array
detail: With n_bootstrap=0 the resample loop is skipped and np.percentile(empty, q) raises on a zero-size array (np.mean also warns NaN). No validation on n_bootstrap>=1 nor ci_level in (0,1) (out-of-range ci_level silently yields wrong percentiles).
fix: Validate n_bootstrap>=1 and 0<ci_level<1 up front with ValueError naming the parameter; add tests for both.

title: detect_cat_columns polars branch omits the int-as-cat heuristic the pandas branch has
detail: Pandas default candidates (352-359) include integer columns with nunique<=max_unique; polars (340-347) takes only non-numeric columns -- low-card Int columns are never cat candidates on polars frames. Exactly the asymmetry FUw3a1 fixed for detect_group_column_candidates; its parity test covers only the group detector.
fix: Mirror the group-detector parity block (int dtypes + n_unique<=max_unique) in detect_cat_columns' polars branch; extend the FUw3a1 parity test to detect_cat_columns.

title: Boolean columns excluded by pandas branches but included by polars branches; docstring promises bools
detail: is_numeric_dtype(bool)=True and is_integer_dtype(bool)=False, so bools fail both clauses of the pandas default-candidate filters in detect_group_column_candidates (215-219) and detect_cat_columns (352-359); polars treats Boolean as non-numeric and includes them. detect_cat_columns docstring claims defaults cover 'str / bool / low-card int' -- wrong for pandas.
fix: Add or pd.api.types.is_bool_dtype(df[c]) to both pandas candidate filters; parity test with a bool column on both frame flavours.

title: detect_cat_columns score increases monotonically with cardinality: ID-like columns rank first
detail: info_bonus=n/(log1p(n)+1) is strictly increasing, so score=coverage_top10*info_bonus rewards 500-1000-level columns over 10-100-level ones (a 500-level near-ID scores ~34 vs ~10 for a clean 50-level cat), contradicting the in-code comment 'rewards 10-100 categories more than 2 or 500'. Classic int-ID false positive for FHC target encoding.
fix: Make the bonus unimodal (peak ~30-100 levels) or multiply by a sparsity penalty min(1, median_per_cat/(5*min_samples_per_cat)); biz_value test: 40-level true categorical must outrank an 800-level pseudo-ID.

title: Strict monotonicity (diffs > 0) rejects timestamp columns with duplicate timestamps
detail: Numeric time-key detection requires strictly increasing/decreasing values; duplicated timestamps (multiple events per tick -- the norm in event data) fail np.all(diffs>0) and the column is silently not a candidate, while the sibling _is_monotone_nondecreasing accepts ties. Int/float epoch columns (the documented 'timestamp-as-int' pattern) are the casualties.
fix: Accept weak monotonicity (diffs>=0 with at least one >0, i.e. non-constant) keeping direction reporting; optionally score strict monotone above tied.

title: Datetime columns pass the detect_cat_columns candidate filter: target-encoding a date is a time-leak trap
detail: Datetime dtypes are neither float nor numeric in pandas/polars, so they land in detect_cat_columns' default candidates; ~100 distinct dates x >=20 rows scores high as a 'categorical' whose FHC target-mean/WoE encoding memorises per-period target means -- out-of-time generalisation poison, inconsistent with the module's own time/group separation.
fix: Exclude temporal dtypes (datetime/date/timestamp/duration) from default candidates of detect_cat_columns and detect_group_column_candidates; keep reachable via explicit candidate_columns.

title: cv_selector_mode config honoured by forward stepwise but silently ignored by the tiny rerank
detail: _fit.py:682-685 threads cv_selector_mode/alpha/confidence/quantile_level into forward_stepwise_multi_base, but the rerank's four tiny-CV call sites pass none of them despite _tiny_cv_rmse_* exposing the params -- aggregation silently stays 'mean'. A user setting t_lcb gets penalty-aware selection in one phase and plain means in the adjacent phase.
fix: Thread the four cv_selector_* config fields into all four rerank call sites (composite+raw, wilcoxon+plain), same getattr pattern as _fit.py:682-685.

title: CatBoost tiny model missing allow_writing_files=False: parallel fold fits race on catboost_info/
detail: _build_tiny_model('catboost') sets verbose=False only; CatBoost still writes catboost_info/ learn logs to CWD per fit. The rerank runs many fits concurrently (threads over specs/folds) -> concurrent writers to one directory; Windows file-lock errors are swallowed by the blanket fold except into silent NaN folds, quietly reducing effective fold count, plus CWD litter.
fix: Add allow_writing_files=False (and a temp train_dir) to the CatBoost kwargs in _build_tiny_model.

title: Zero-base baseline sentinel std(y) is not CV-honest
detail: Empty-base baseline returns full-sample np.std(y) while candidates are scored by out-of-fold RMSE; the honest comparator is CV-RMSE of the train-fold-mean predictor (systematically higher, much higher under TSS on trending y). Affects only the seed_bases=None entry path; biases the first base's relative gain.
fix: Compute the 0-base baseline through the same kf.split loop predicting mean(y[train_fold]) per fold.

title: Degenerate single-seed + wilcoxon + per-bin path unpacks the per-seed array as the per-bin array
detail: When _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale early-returns bare NaN, the single-seed wrapper returns (nan, per_seed_arr); _rerank_one_spec's wilcoxon branch reads result[1] as per_bin_first -> empty per-seed array cached as a per-bin breakdown (passes 'is not None', blocks the fallback recompute). Benign today (spec already inf-scored) but wrong typing and fragile.
fix: Normalise the degenerate single-seed return to the full (nan, full(n_bins, nan), per_seed) shape.

title: Conjugate bayesian_alpha_fit lacks the caller-must-filter-NaN contract its bootstrap sibling documents
detail: NaN in y/base silently yields NaN coef/posterior (or the degenerate-inf dict via the LinAlgError path) with no warning. The bootstrap variant documents 'Caller must filter to the valid domain'; the conjugate variant, documented as a drop-in swap, does not.
fix: Copy the contract line into the conjugate docstring, or add a one-line joint finite-mask in both variants.

title: Polars n_unique() counts null; pandas nunique() excludes NaN: off-by-one parity at max_unique
detail: In the default-candidate gates of detect_group_column_candidates (polars line 205 vs pandas line 218), an int column with exactly max_unique non-null values plus nulls passes on pandas and fails on polars.
fix: Use drop_nulls().n_unique() in the polars gates.

title: Candidate array lengths documented but never validated
detail: Docstring requires all candidate arrays to have len(y_train); a mismatched array surfaces later as an opaque column_stack ValueError (or wrong folds if broadcastable). No up-front check naming the offending column.
fix: One-line validation loop raising ValueError with the offending column name and lengths.

title: No end-to-end test trains stacked pass-2 specs through the suite; no Wilcoxon power-floor or multiseed-variation test
detail: Existing tests assert the phase invokes fit_stacked_on_residual and specs return; nothing asserts merged pass-2 specs train downstream with a non-NaN base / correct target scale (would catch A6/A7). No test runs use_wilcoxon_gate=True at default seeds (A2) or pins that multiseed under a monotone base yields seed-to-seed variation (A3).
fix: Add: (1) biz_value end-to-end stacked discovery asserting a finite non-constant composite target trains; (2) unit: wilcoxon gate with 3 seeds raises/warns; (3) unit: multiseed + monotone base yields >1 distinct per-seed value or short-circuits to 1 repeat.
